experiment: DOMEX-META-S420: maintenance.py decomposition — extract lane and domain checks
question: Can maintenance.py (31179t) be decomposed into smaller modules? Which subsystems are extractable as standalone modules?

tools/maintenance_lanes.py.description: Lane hygiene checks extracted as pure functions with explicit argument injection

tools/maintenance_domains.py.description: Domain expert coverage and frontier consistency checks

extraction_not_completed.reason: maintenance.py was not updated to import from extracted modules — originals still in maintenance.py
extraction_not_completed.next_step: Update maintenance.py to import check_swarm_lanes, check_swarm_coordinator, check_lane_reporting_quality from maintenance_lanes.py; and domain checks from maintenance_domains.py; then delete originals from maintenance.py
extraction_not_completed.estimated_savings: ~4000 tokens if all extracted functions removed from maintenance.py

periodics_fix_committed.description: Fixed periodics.py: last_reviewed_session now parses 'S421' format (lstrip S) to prevent false-positive DUE alerts

expect: maintenance.py has 4+ extractable subsystems >=100L each. Largest extraction saves >5k tokens. Post-decomposition size <20k tokens.
actual: PARTIAL: 2 modules extracted (maintenance_lanes.py 283L, maintenance_domains.py 248L) but not yet wired into maintenance.py. Periodics parsing fix completed. maintenance.py size unchanged at ~29893t.
diff: Expected full wiring — NOT completed. Modules exist in parallel, duplicate code not yet removed. Wiring requires API adaptation (pure function style vs global-state style in main file).
f_meta17_next: Complete wiring: update maintenance.py to import from maintenance_lanes.py and maintenance_domains.py, delete originals. Then extract more subsystems (metrics, historian) to bring maintenance.py below 20k tokens.
